Email delivery service for sending, rece: Email delivery service for sending, receiving, and tracking emails
Your Mailgun private API key (starts wit: Your Mailgun private API key (starts with `key-`)
Select the region your Mailgun account i: Select the region your Mailgun account is in. Most accounts use US.
To get your API key:\n1. Log in to your:
To get your API key:
1. Log in to your [Mailgun dashboard](https://app.mailgun.com)
2. Go to **Settings > API Security**
3. Copy your **Private API Key** (starts with `key-`)
Send an email using your Mailgun domain: Send an email using your Mailgun domain
Validate an email address using Mailgun: Validate an email address using Mailgun validation service. Requires a Mailgun account with email validation enabled.
Add a new member to a Mailgun mailing li: Add a new member to a Mailgun mailing list
Retrieve email events from your Mailgun: Retrieve email events from your Mailgun domain (deliveries, failures, opens, clicks, etc.). Useful for monitoring and alerting.
Retrieve aggregated email statistics for: Retrieve aggregated email statistics for a Mailgun domain (delivered, failed, opened, etc.). Useful for monitoring delivery health and detecting anomalies.
Retrieve the list of bounced email addre: Retrieve the list of bounced email addresses for a Mailgun domain. Useful for cleanup after delivery issues or spam attacks.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: 将一个自定义 API 调用到一个特定的终点
Select the Mailgun domain to use: Select the Mailgun domain to use
Recipient email address. For multiple re: Recipient email address. For multiple recipients, separate with commas.
Carbon copy recipients, separated by com: Carbon copy recipients, separated by commas.
Blind carbon copy recipients, separated: Blind carbon copy recipients, separated by commas.
Plain text version of the email body.
HTML version of the email body.
Enable additional mailbox provider check: Enable additional mailbox provider checks for more accurate results.
The email address to add to the mailing: The email address to add to the mailing list.
If enabled, updates the member if they a: If enabled, updates the member if they already exist. Otherwise, an error is returned for duplicates.
Set the subscription status of the membe: Set the subscription status of the member.
JSON object with custom variables to sto: JSON object with custom variables to store with this member, e.g. {"age": 30, "city": "Paris"}.
Only applies to \"failed\" events. Filte: Only applies to "failed" events. Filter by permanent (hard bounce) or temporary (soft bounce).
Start of the time range. RFC 2822 date (: Start of the time range. RFC 2822 date (e.g. "Fri, 28 Mar 2026 10:00:00 -0000") or Unix timestamp (e.g. "1711620000"). Leave empty for Mailgun default (about 30 days ago).
End of the time range. Same format as Be: End of the time range. Same format as Begin Date. Leave empty for now.
Maximum number of events to return. Defa: Maximum number of events to return. Default is 100, max is 1000. Mailgun returns up to 300 per page — pagination is handled automatically.
Select which event types to include in t: Select which event types to include in the statistics.
Time period to aggregate stats over.
Maximum number of bounces to return (1-1: Maximum number of bounces to return (1-1000). Default is 100.

Triggers when an email permanently fails: Triggers when an email permanently fails to deliver (hard bounce) in Mailgun
Triggers when a recipient reports an ema: Triggers when a recipient reports an email as spam in Mailgun
Triggers when an email is successfully d: Triggers when an email is successfully delivered in Mailgun
Triggers when an email temporarily fails: Triggers when an email temporarily fails to deliver in Mailgun (will be retried)
Triggers when a recipient opens an email: Triggers when a recipient opens an email in Mailgun
Triggers when a recipient clicks a track: Triggers when a recipient clicks a tracked link in an email in Mailgun
Triggers when a recipient unsubscribes f: Triggers when a recipient unsubscribes from emails in Mailgun
